title: Circuit of the Americas
sub: A Tilke greatest-hits circuit in the Texas hills: a steep uphill blast to a hairpin Turn 1, a Silverstone-style ess sequence, an Istanbul-inspired multi-apex left, and a stadium section. Fast, flowing and a proper driver’s track.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: The steep uphill launch to the blind Turn 1 hairpin, then the plunge into the fast, flowing Turn 3-6 esses inspired by Silverstone’s Maggotts-Becketts.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: The Turn 11 hairpin at the end of the back straight (prime passing), then the Istanbul-style multi-apex Turn 16-18 approach and the tight stadium section.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: The long triple-apex Turn 16-17-18 left, then the technical final corners back onto the main straight and the climb to Turn 1.

tyreAlloc: Middle of the range · C2 / C3 / C4
stratNote: The fast, sustained-load esses and a notoriously bumpy, subsiding surface stress the tyres and the drivers’ backs alike. Degradation, and how the bumps knock the car around, drive the one-versus-two-stop call.
spots: The uphill hairpin at the top of the climb off the main straight + DRS. A classic late-brake lunge.
End of the long back straight + DRS, heavy braking into the stadium. The best passing spot.
A secondary dive on the exit of the stadium section.
otNote: Two DRS zones, a steep-braking Turn 1 and a long back straight into Turn 12 give plenty of chances. The fast esses hurt following, but the two big stops make it a reliably racy circuit.
corners: Steep blind climb off the main straight into a slow left hairpin + DRS. The signature COTA braking zone.
Downhill right dropping into the esses.
The Silverstone-inspired left-right-left-right at ~250 km/h. The heart of the lap.
Continuing flowing sequence toward the far loop.
Quick left onto the run to the hairpin.
Slow left setting up the back straight.
Tight left hairpin; exit speed feeds the long back straight.
Heavy braking at the end of the back straight + DRS. The prime overtaking spot.
Slow corners through the packed stadium section.
Medium right opening the final sector.
A long, Istanbul-style multi-apex left taken at high, sustained speed.
Quick technical flick back onto the main straight and the climb to Turn 1.
geo: A 22m climb from the grid to a blind hairpin, one of the most dramatic braking zones in F1. Cars disappear over the crest on the run down to the esses.
Tilke stitched in tributes: Silverstone’s Maggotts-Becketts esses, Istanbul’s multi-apex Turn 8, Hockenheim’s stadium. A greatest-hits lap that still flows as its own thing.
Built on shifting Texas clay, COTA has subsided and bumped up over the years, jarring the cars and the drivers’ backs. Resurfacing has helped but the bumps persist.
The USA’s first purpose-built F1 track (2012) and the anchor of F1’s American boom, now one of three US rounds alongside Miami and Las Vegas.
